detail: initial model from ideation phase scoring
D1 5→7: 102 CASPs authorized (Dec 2025), 150-180 expected by mid-2026. TAM clears >=100 threshold.
B1 6→5: Sedric AI already does MiCA marketing compliance. Shifted wedge to disclosure generation. C2 7→6: new wedge unvalidated.
C2 6→5: White paper generation is one-time deliverable, poor SaaS fit. Shifted wedge to ongoing compliance operations platform.
C2 5→7: Recurring MiCA obligations validated (record-keeping, complaints, NCA reporting, annual audits). Operations wedge has SaaS potential.
D2 7→8: 35% of startups estimate >$500K/yr compliance cost. 73% investing in compliance tech. EUR 999/mo is 2-6% of compliance budget.
E2 8→9: MiCA NOT one-time wave. CARF/DAC8, PSD2 interplay, AMLA, AMLR layer through 2027. 30+ ESMA standards still rolling out.
E1 9→8: Stress test. Jeroen's network is 5-10 intros not full CASP landscape. AI capability is table-stakes. No compliance domain expertise.
B1 5→6, B2 6→7: Validated gap in integrated MiCA ops (NCA reporting + change tracking + multi-reg coordination). Narrowed wedge to regulatory change tracking.
C1 8→7: Legal AI error rates 17-34% (Stanford). EU AI Act mandates human oversight. Must be hybrid AI+human, not pure AI.
A1 8→9: 40%+ struggle with reporting, 75% revamped teams, 25% plan EU exit. Pain is existential for mid-market CASPs.
A2 8→9: Regulatory expansion structural — MiCA L2/3 + CARF + AMLA + AMLR + MiCA II on horizon. Multi-year compound growth.
D1 stress test held at 7. Of 150-180 CASPs, ~100-130 are mid-market after filtering out large exchanges and micro-firms.
C2 stress test held at 7. Regulatory change tracking wedge is recurring and specific but broad platform risk remains.
B1 stress test held at 6. Horizontal compliance platforms (ComplyAdvantage, Alloy) could enter. B1 ceiling at 6 without paying customers.
Score plateau confirmed at 77/100. All desk-researchable improvements exhausted.
